key_concerns: Higher rate of early deaths in cilta-cel arm
Crossing OS curves indicating non-proportional hazards
Unclear overall benefit-risk due to safety concerns
Inability to identify patient subgroups to mitigate risk
Heavy censoring limits OS interpretation after 11 months
key_positives: Statistically significant and clinically meaningful PFS improvement (HR 0.41)
Overall AE rates comparable, with some toxicities lower in cilta-cel
Unmet medical need in relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma

evidence_notes: Treatment with cilta-cel demonstrated a statistically significant improvement in PFS as assessed by IRC; HR 0.41 (95% CI: 0.30, 0.56; p-value <0.0001)
Higher rate of early deaths associated with ciltacabtagene-autoleucel treatment
Crossing hazards pattern favoring the standard therapy arm up to approximately 11 months
More deaths due to AEs occurred in the cilta-cel arm (11%) compared to the standard therapy arm (8%)
The rate of death in the first 10 months post randomization was higher in the cilta-cel arm (29 of 208; 14%) than in the standard therapy arm (25 of 211; 12%)
Exploratory, retrospective, data-informed analyses... may be limited in providing definitive evidence to support potential risk mitigation strategies
key_concerns: Higher rate of early deaths in cilta-cel arm
Crossing OS curves indicating non-proportional hazards
Unclear overall benefit-risk due to safety concerns
Inability to identify patient subgroups to mitigate risk
Heavy censoring limits OS interpretation after 11 months
key_positives: Statistically significant and clinically meaningful PFS improvement (HR 0.41)
Overall AE rates comparable, with some toxicities lower in cilta-cel
Unmet medical need in relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma

questions_framing.question: Discuss whether the results are sufficient to support a positive risk-benefit assessment, specifically if the risk of early death is acceptable.

document.title: March 15, 2024 Meeting of the Oncologic Drugs Advisory Committee- FDA Briefing Document (CARVYKTI)

signal.label: MIXED
rationale: Concerns: Higher rate of early deaths in cilta-cel arm; Crossing OS curves indicating non-proportional hazards; Unclear overall benefit-risk due to safety concerns; Inability to identify patient subgroups to mitigate risk; Heavy censoring limits OS interpretation after 11 months

Positives: Statistically significant and clinically meaningful PFS improvement (HR 0.41); High response rates (CR/sCR 74% vs 22%); Overall AE rates comparable, with some toxicities lower in cilta-cel; Unmet medical need in relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma
